title: Why "No Bill But SB13" ?
author: James Silberman
orientation: James Silberman explains why Free the States exclusively supports Oklahoma's Senate Bill 13 (the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act) by laying out the five components of a true abolition bill, dismantling rival 'fake abolition' bills, and arguing that incremental pro-life legislation is counterproductive. The piece grounds the strategy in scripture, abolitionist history (Heyrick, Garrison, the American Anti-Slavery Society), and the doctrine of the lesser magistrate.
audiences: Pro-life Christian asking whether incremental bills are 'a step in the right direction'
Oklahoma legislator weighing SB13 against SB1721 or HB1182
Pastor or church leader trying to discern faithful action on abortion
Pro-life lobbyist defending current regulatory laws
Abolitionist activist learning to articulate the case for SB13

questions.question: What is the scriptural foundation Free the States invokes to justify defying Roe v. Wade?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader trying to discern faithful action on abortion
questions.answer: Silberman opens with Acts 5:29, framing SB13 as Oklahoma's institutional act of obeying God rather than men in the face of a Supreme Court order to allow mass murder. He treats Roe as a direct command to neglect the biblical duty to love one's neighbor and establish justice for the fatherless.
questions.quote: When the government demands we choose between obedience to man or obedience to God, scripture is perfectly clear about what we are to do. In Acts 5, Peter and the apostles get into trouble for teaching in the name of Jesus. Verse 29 records their response to the governing authorities: "But Peter and the Apostles answered, 'We must obey God rather than men.'"

questions.question: What are the five components that make a bill a true bill of abolition?
questions.audience: Oklahoma legislator weighing SB13 against SB1721 or HB1182
questions.answer: SB13 (1) recognizes life begins at conception and grants equal legal protection from that moment, (2) makes no exceptions for murder including rape or life-of-the-mother, (3) criminalizes abortion equally for all participants including the mother, (4) is not contingent on Supreme Court approval and contains nullification language, and (5) repeals existing pro-life regulations because those regulations codify the legality of abortion under certain conditions.
questions.quote: Here are the five components of SB13 that make it consistent with these verses and make it a bona fide bill of total and immediate abolition.

questions.question: Why does SB13 refuse the standard pro-life exception for pregnancies conceived in rape?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christian asking whether incremental bills are 'a step in the right direction'
questions.answer: Silberman argues that the child conceived in rape is the most fatherless person imaginable and that punishing the child for the father's crime is exactly the iniquitous decree Isaiah 10 condemns. He further contends that any exception undermines the entire moral case against abortion, because murder cannot stop being murder based on the circumstances of conception.
questions.quote: There is no one more fatherless than the child conceived in rape. It is unacceptable to allow for the murder of a baby because of the crime of the father. Furthermore, allowances for abortion in certain scenarios undermine the whole case against abortion.

questions.question: Why does SB13 criminalize the mother rather than granting her automatic immunity like most pro-life bills?
questions.audience: Pro-life lobbyist defending current regulatory laws
questions.answer: Silberman cites Justice Blackmun's footnote 54 in Roe, which held that Texas's automatic immunity for mothers proved Texas did not really view the preborn as persons. Granting immunity, he argues, both denies the preborn equal protection and effectively legalizes self-induced abortion via abortion pills.
questions.quote: To write a law outlawing abortion but giving automatic immunity to the parents means that the mother is free to induce her own abortion. SB13 does not fail to provide equal protection to preborn humans and therefore does not legalize self-induced abortion.

questions.question: How does SB13 handle the Supreme Court's authority over state abortion law?
questions.audience: Oklahoma legislator weighing SB13 against SB1721 or HB1182
questions.answer: SB13 nullifies Roe rather than submitting to it, leaning on the doctrine of the lesser magistrate: state executives, legislators, and judges are obligated to interpose between abortion victims and a tyrannical federal judiciary. The bill includes explicit language voiding any contrary federal statute, regulation, executive order, or court decision.
questions.quote: Any federal statute, regulation, executive order or court decision which purports to supersede, stay or overrule this Act is in violation of the Constitution of the State of Oklahoma and the Constitution of the United States of America and is therefore void.

questions.question: Why must SB13 repeal Oklahoma's existing pro-life laws like the partial-birth abortion ban and parental notification?
questions.audience: Pro-life lobbyist defending current regulatory laws
questions.answer: Silberman argues every pro-life regulation codifies the legality of murdering a preborn child under specified conditions: parental notification legalizes abortion when grandparents are informed, the dismemberment ban legalizes abortion when a different technique is used, and so on. Because these statutes contradict an outright abolition of abortion as murder, they must be repealed for SB13 to function.
questions.quote: Abortion cannot be abolished while there are still laws on the books saying abortion is legal as long as you jump through pro-life hoops.

questions.question: Could a court strike down only the abolition portions of SB13 while keeping the repeals, leaving Oklahoma with no abortion regulations at all?
questions.audience: Oklahoma legislator weighing SB13 against SB1721 or HB1182
questions.answer: No. Section 34 of SB13 declares the bill's provisions inseverable, so a judge cannot uphold the repeals while striking the abolition. Silberman accuses Tony Lauinger and the Oklahoma Baptists of repeating this claim despite being directly informed it is false.
questions.quote: Section 34 of SB13 reads "The provisions, words, phrases, and clauses of this act are declared to be inseverable." A judge could not strike down parts of the bill while upholding others.

questions.question: What is the problem with Senator Bullard's SB1721, the 'Unborn Person Equality Act'?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist learning to articulate the case for SB13
questions.answer: SB1721 begins with strong language about life beginning at conception but caveats Part C with a clause subjecting all of its protections to Supreme Court 'decisional interpretations,' which renders the entire bill contingent on a court that has reaffirmed Roe thirty-seven times. Silberman frames this as obeying men while merely asking the court for permission to obey God.
questions.quote: Where SB13 says, "We will obey God rather than a supreme court opinion that Oklahoma must allow the mass murder of preborn humans," SB1721 says, "We will obey men rather than God when the supreme court opines that we should allow the mass murder of preborn humans, but we ask the supreme court to let us obey God."

questions.question: Why does Silberman call HB1182 (Rep. Jim Olsen's medical-license bill) 'legal nonsense'?
questions.audience: Oklahoma legislator weighing SB13 against SB1721 or HB1182
questions.answer: HB1182 only strips a doctor's medical license and adds a small criminal penalty without nullifying Roe, so it cannot take effect without court approval and undermines its own moral logic by treating abortion as a licensing infraction rather than murder. A state cannot coherently revoke a license for an act that state law still permits.
questions.quote: How can a state take away a medical license from a doctor for performing certain acts when that same state's laws say that the act is legal? And how can a state argue that abortion is murder if the penalty for doing it is a mere loss of license and a possible one-to-three year sentence and $500 fine? HB1182 is legal nonsense that undermines itself and would not abolish abortion.

questions.question: Why are incremental pro-life bills not merely insufficient but actively counterproductive?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christian asking whether incremental bills are 'a step in the right direction'
questions.answer: Incremental bills give politicians like Greg Treat cover to refuse abolition, codify the supreme court's claim of authority over the question, and teach the public dehumanizing falsehoods. Silberman cites a young woman at a Norman killing center who believed her abortion was acceptable because the Pain-Capable Unborn Child Protection Act had taught her babies who can't feel pain may be killed.
questions.quote: Incremental pro-life bills are not simply less good than abolition bills. They are counterproductive. They are the excuse politicians use to not abolish abortion. They instill in the culture dehumanizing misinformation about the preborn and the idolatrous idea that we owe unconditional submission to the supreme court.

questions.question: What historical abolitionist precedent does Silberman draw on to attack the strategy of gradual abolition?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist learning to articulate the case for SB13
questions.answer: He marshals the American Anti-Slavery Society's 1833 Declaration of Sentiments, William Lloyd Garrison's 'No compromise with slavery!' and Elizabeth Heyrick's pamphlet Immediate, Not Gradual Abolition. Heyrick's argument is that gradualism originated with the slaveholder himself as a satanic device to dull the moral outrage that would otherwise destroy the system.
questions.quote: But this GRADUAL ABOLITION, has been the grand marplot of human virtue and happiness; the very masterpiece of satanic policy. By converting the cry for immediate, into gradual emancipation, the prince of slave holders, 'transformed himself, with astonishing dexterity, into an angel of light,' and thereby 'deceived the very elect.'

questions.question: What evidence does Silberman offer that a 'paradigm shift' toward abolition is underway?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader trying to discern faithful action on abortion
questions.answer: He points to three concrete actions in Oklahoma: the Free Will Baptists' May 2019 unanimous resolution authored by Pastor Tim Gillespie, the Oklahoma Baptists' (SBC affiliate) November 12, 2019 platform amendment introduced by Pastor Bill Ascol calling for the immediate end of abortion, and the Oklahoma Republican Party amending its platform to call for an SB13 floor vote.
questions.quote: These resolutions are the result of a paradigm shift that is taking place toward rejecting compromises with abortion and demanding the killing be stopped immediately. This paradigm shift is happening around the country, but is especially far along in Oklahoma.

questions.question: What does Silberman say is the actual obstacle to passing SB13 in Oklahoma?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader trying to discern faithful action on abortion
questions.answer: Republicans hold supermajorities in both chambers, so the votes exist; the obstacle is the political choice to pass incremental regulations instead of abolition, and the silence of the church. Silberman quotes a December 2019 conversation with a legislator who said 10,000 Oklahomans showing up at a rally would force passage.
questions.quote: In a state like Oklahoma where pro-life Republicans hold super-majorities in the Senate and the House, it's not that we cannot pass a bill abolishing abortion because we do not have the votes, it's that we could pass a bill abolishing abortion but choose to pass pro-life bills regulating abortion instead.

questions.question: What is the church's specific obligation in a culture practicing industrial-scale child sacrifice?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader trying to discern faithful action on abortion
questions.answer: Silberman invokes R.C. Sproul's warning that a silent church in the midst of holocaust ceases to be a real church, then turns to Isaiah 1:12-17 to show God's refusal to hear the prayers of those whose hands are full of blood. The remedy is to seek justice, correct oppression, and bring justice to the fatherless.
questions.quote: A Christian who lives in a culture that practices child sacrifice on an industrial scale is obligated by the law of love to demand the abolition of that practice. It must be demanded with passion and it must be demanded by all.

related.title: Immediate, Not Gradual Abolition of Abortion
related.reason: Silberman's longer treatment of the Heyrick/Garrison case against gradualism that this article quotes from.

related.title: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Names Tony Lauinger and the Oklahoma pro-life leaders the SB13 piece accuses of spreading falsehoods about the bill.

related.title: The Inexhaustible Cowardice of Oklahoma Senate President Greg Treat
related.reason: Backstory on the Treat/Smalley denial of SB13 a hearing in 2019, the central case study in the 'No Compromise' section.

related.title: Pastor and Gubernatorial Candidate Dan Fisher Responds to Oklahoma Baptist Leaders Opposing SB13
related.reason: Direct rebuttal of the Baptist General Convention of Oklahoma leaders quoted in this article on the repeal-of-pro-life-laws objection.

related.title: Oklahoma Baptists Pass Resolution Demanding an Immediate "End" to Abortion
related.reason: Coverage of the November 2019 Oklahoma Baptists resolution Silberman cites as evidence of the paradigm shift.

related.title: Uncovered Letter Exposes Plan to Keep Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Documents the institutional opposition to SB13 from Oklahoma pro-life leadership referenced throughout this piece.

related.title: Are Nullification's Advocates the "Villains" of American History?
related.reason: Develops the lesser-magistrate / nullification doctrine that underpins SB13's refusal to seek Supreme Court approval.
